summary_en: An involuntary musical image that loops in your mind for hours or days — your brain's prediction engine replaying a melodic fragment it hasn't yet 'resolved'.
summary_tr: Saatlerce veya gunlerce zihninizde dongu yapan istemsiz bir muzikal imge — beyninizin tahmin motorunun henuz 'cozumleyemedigi' bir melodik parcayi tekrar oynatmasi.
deep_en: Williamson 2012: Earworms (involuntary musical imagery, INMI) activate auditory cortex, SMA, and IFG without external input. Linked to heightened DA wanting-loop without opioidergic consummation. Musical features that increase INMI probability: simple pitch contour, moderate tempo (100-130 BPM), rising intervals. The phonological loop in working memory sustains the repetition.
deep_tr: Williamson 2012: Kulak kurtlari (istemsiz muzikal imgelem, INMI) dis girdi olmadan isitsel korteks, SMA ve IFG'yi aktive eder. Opioiderjik tuketim olmadan yukselmis DA istek dongusuyle baglantili. INMI olasiligini artiran muzikal ozellikler: basit perde konturu, orta tempo (100-130 BPM), yukselen araliklar. Calisma bellegindeki fonolojik dongu tekrari surdurmektedir.
fun_fact_en: The most 'sticky' earworm songs share a common recipe: slightly unusual intervals combined with a very simple rhythm. That's why 'Bad Guy' and 'Bohemian Rhapsody' top the earworm charts.
fun_fact_tr: En 'yapisan' kulak kurdu sarkilari ortak bir recete paylasiyor: cok basit bir ritimle birlestirilmis hafifce olagandisi araliklar. Bu yuzden 'Bad Guy' ve 'Bohemian Rhapsody' kulak kurdu listelerinin basinda.

summary_en: The rare ability to identify or produce any musical pitch without an external reference note — present in roughly 1 in 10,000 people, with higher prevalence among tonal-language speakers.
summary_tr: Harici bir referans nota olmadan herhangi bir muzikal perdeyi tanimlama veya uretme konusundaki nadir yetenek — kabaca 10.000 kisiden 1'inde bulunur, tonal dil konusanlarinda daha yaygindir.
deep_en: Zatorre 2003: AP possessors show enlarged left planum temporale and reduced right-hemispheric activation during pitch naming. Critical period before age 6 appears necessary but not sufficient. Genetic component (EPHA7 locus) interacts with early exposure. AP holders rely on categorical pitch memory rather than relative interval processing.
deep_tr: Zatorre 2003: Mutlak perde sahipleri perde isimlendirme sirasinda buyumus sol planum temporale ve azalmis sag yarikure aktivasyonu gosterir. 6 yasindan once kritik donem gerekli gorunur ama yeterli degildir. Genetik bilesen (EPHA7 lokusu) erken maruziyetle etkilesir. MP sahipleri goreceli aralik islemesi yerine kategorik perde bellegine dayanir.
fun_fact_en: In Japan and China, where tonal languages use pitch to distinguish word meaning, absolute pitch is up to 8 times more common than in Western populations.
fun_fact_tr: Japonya ve Cin'de, tonal dillerin kelime anlamini ayirt etmek icin perde kullandigi yerlerde, mutlak perde algisi Bati popülasyonlarina gore 8 kat daha yaygindir.

summary_en: A lifelong inability to perceive or produce music normally, despite intact hearing and cognition — often called 'tone deafness', affecting about 4% of the population.
summary_tr: Normal isitme ve bilise ragmen muzigi normal sekilde algilayamama veya uretememe — genellikle 'ton sagirligi' olarak adlandirilir, nufusun yaklasik %4'unu etkiler.
deep_en: Peretz 2001: Congenital amusia involves impaired fine-grained pitch discrimination (>2 semitone threshold vs <1 semitone in neurotypicals). White matter disconnection between right IFG and auditory cortex (arcuate fasciculus). Rhythm perception often spared. Montreal Battery of Evaluation of Amusia (MBEA) is the standard diagnostic.
deep_tr: Peretz 2001: Dogumsal amuzi bozulmus ince taneli perde ayrimi icerir (norotipiklerde <1 yari ton esigine karsi >2 yari ton esigi). Sag IFG ile isitsel korteks arasinda beyaz madde baglanti kopuklugu (arkuat fasikulus). Ritim algisi genellikle korunur. Montreal Amuzi Degerlendirme Bataryasi (MBEA) standart tani aracidir.
fun_fact_en: Most people who think they're tone deaf actually aren't — true congenital amusia is rare and involves a measurable brain wiring difference, not just lack of training.
fun_fact_tr: Ton sagiri oldugunu dusunen cogu kisi aslinda degildir — gercek dogumsal amuzi nadirdir ve sadece egitim eksikligi degil, olculebilir bir beyin baglanti farkliligini icerir.

summary_en: The optimal psychological state of complete immersion where self-awareness dissolves, time distorts, and action flows effortlessly — music is one of the most reliable triggers.
summary_tr: Oz farkindaligin eridigi, zamanin bozuldugu ve eylemin zahmetsizce aktigi tam dalginlik optimal psikolojik durumu — muzik en guvenilir tetikleyicilerden biridir.
deep_en: Csikszentmihalyi 1990: Flow requires skill-challenge balance, clear goals, immediate feedback. During musical flow, prefrontal cortex shows transient hypofrontality (reduced self-monitoring). DMN and task-positive networks decouple. DA and endocannabinoid release maintain the state. Musical improvisation shows maximal flow-related brain patterns (Limb & Braun 2008).
deep_tr: Csikszentmihalyi 1990: Akis, beceri-zorluk dengesi, net hedefler, aninda geribildirim gerektirir. Muzikal akis sirasinda prefrontal korteks gecici hipofrontalite gosterir (azalmis oz-izleme). DMN ve gorev-pozitif aglar ayrisir. DA ve endokannabinoid salinimi durumu surdurmektedir. Muzikal dogaclama maksimum akisla iliskili beyin oruntuleri gosterir (Limb & Braun 2008).
fun_fact_en: Jazz musicians in flow actually turn OFF parts of their prefrontal cortex — their brain literally stops self-censoring so creativity can pour out.
fun_fact_tr: Akis durumundaki caz muzisyenleri prefrontal kortekslerinin bazi bolumlerini gercekten KAPATIR — beyinleri yaraticilik akabilsin diye kelimenin tam anlamiyla oz-sansuru durdurur.

summary_en: A neurological condition where musical sounds automatically trigger experiences in other senses — seeing colors, tasting shapes, or feeling textures from specific notes or keys.
summary_tr: Muzikal seslerin otomatik olarak diger duyularda deneyimler tetikledigi norolojik bir durum — belirli notalardan veya anahtarlardan renkler gorme, sekiller tatma veya dokular hissetme.
deep_en: Ward 2006: Chromesthesia (sound→color) is the most common musical synesthesia subtype, affecting ~1 in 3,000. Involves hyperconnectivity between auditory cortex and V4 color area. Associations are consistent within individuals across decades. Diffusion tensor imaging shows increased white-matter connectivity in synesthetes. Genetic basis with incomplete penetrance.
deep_tr: Ward 2006: Kromestezi (ses→renk) en yaygin muzikal sinestezi alt tipidir, ~3.000 kisiden 1'ini etkiler. Isitsel korteks ile V4 renk alani arasinda asiri baglantiyi icerir. Iliskilendirmeler bireyler icinde on yillar boyunca tutarlidir. Difuzyon tensor goruntuleme sinesteziklerde artmis beyaz madde baglantisi gosterir. Eksik penetransli genetik temel.
fun_fact_en: Pharrell Williams, Lady Gaga, and Billie Eilish all report seeing colors when they hear music — synesthesia may actually be an advantage for musical creativity.
fun_fact_tr: Pharrell Williams, Lady Gaga ve Billie Eilish muzik duyduklarinda renkler gorduklerini bildiriyor — sinestezi muzikal yaraticilik icin aslinda bir avantaj olabilir.

summary_en: A tingling sensation that starts at the scalp and moves down the spine, triggered by specific musical textures — soft vocals, gentle plucking, whispery timbres, or delicate high frequencies.
summary_tr: Kafa derisinden baslayip omurga boyunca inen, belirli muzikal dokular tarafindan tetiklenen bir karisma hissi — yumusak vokaller, nazik pena sesleri, fisiltili timbreler veya narin yuksek frekanslar.
deep_en: Lochte et al. 2018: fMRI during ASMR shows activation of medial prefrontal cortex, nucleus accumbens, and insula. Overlaps with but is distinct from musical chills/frisson. ASMR responders show higher scores on openness to experience and increased functional connectivity in DMN. Potential mu-opioid and oxytocin involvement.
deep_tr: Lochte et al. 2018: ASMR sirasinda fMRI medial prefrontal korteks, nukleus akkumbens ve insula aktivasyonu gosterir. Muzikal urperti/frisson ile olusur ama bundan farklidir. ASMR yanitlayicilari deneyime aciklikta daha yuksek puanlar ve DMN'de artmis fonksiyonel baglantilik gosterir. Potansiyel mu-opioid ve oksitosin katilimi.
fun_fact_en: ASMR and musical chills feel similar but use different brain pathways — chills ride dopamine while ASMR seems to involve the opioid-oxytocin 'comfort' system.
fun_fact_tr: ASMR ve muzikal urpertiler benzer hissettrir ama farkli beyin yollari kullanir — urpertiler dopamin uzerinden giderken ASMR opioid-oksitosin 'konfor' sistemini iceriyor gorunur.

summary_en: The frequency-organized layout of the auditory cortex where neighboring neurons respond to neighboring frequencies — a spatial 'piano keyboard' mapped onto brain tissue.
summary_tr: Komsu noronlarin komsu frekanslara yanit verdigi isitsel korteksin frekansa gore duzenlenmis yerlesimi — beyin dokusuna haritalanmis mekansal bir 'piyano klavyesi'.
deep_en: Romani et al. 1982: Tonotopic gradients run from low frequencies (anterolateral) to high frequencies (posteromedial) in primary auditory cortex (A1). In musicians, cortical representation of trained frequency ranges is expanded (Pantev 1998). The tonotopic map is established by cochlear mechanics and refined by thalamocortical projections through the medial geniculate body.
deep_tr: Romani et al. 1982: Tonotopik gradyanlar primer isitsel kortekste (A1) dusuk frekanslardan (anterolateral) yuksek frekanslara (posteromedial) dogru uzanir. Muzisyenlerde egitilmis frekans araliklarinin kortikal temsili genislemistir (Pantev 1998). Tonotopik harita koklear mekaniklerle olusturulur ve medial genikülat cisim yoluyla talamokortikal projeksiyonlarla rafine edilir.
fun_fact_en: Your auditory cortex is literally organized like a piano — low notes are processed on one end, high notes on the other, with every frequency in between mapped in order.
fun_fact_tr: Isitsel korteksiniz kelimenin tam anlamiyla bir piyano gibi duzenlenmistir — dusuk notalar bir ucta, yuksek notalar diger ucta islenir, aradaki her frekans sirali sekilde haritalanmistir.

summary_en: How we distinguish a violin from a trumpet playing the same note — timbre is the 'color' of sound, determined by the unique spectral envelope and temporal attack of each instrument.
summary_tr: Ayni notayi calan bir kemani bir trompetten nasil ayirt ettigimiz — timbre sesin 'rengi'dir, her enstrumanin benzersiz spektral zarfi ve zamansal atagi tarafindan belirlenir.
deep_en: McAdams 2013: Timbre is a multidimensional perceptual attribute with at least 3 principal dimensions: spectral centroid (brightness), spectral flux (temporal variation), and attack time. Processed bilaterally in superior temporal gyrus with right-hemisphere dominance. Planum temporale and anterior STG contribute to timbre categorization. R3 captures this via brightness_kuttruff, spectral_flux, and onset features.
deep_tr: McAdams 2013: Timbre en az 3 ana boyutu olan cok boyutlu bir algisal ozellik: spektral agirlik merkezi (parlaklik), spektral akis (zamansal degisim) ve atak suresi. Superior temporal girusta sag yarikure baskinligi ile bilateral olarak islenir. Planum temporale ve anterior STG timbre siniflndirmasina katkilar. R3 bunu brightness_kuttruff, spectral_flux ve onset ozellikleri araciligiyla yakalar.
fun_fact_en: A computer can identify an instrument from its timbre in milliseconds, but it still can't explain WHY a Stradivarius sounds different from an ordinary violin — that mystery involves wood, varnish, and 300 years of aging.
fun_fact_tr: Bir bilgisayar bir enstrumani tinisindan milisaniyeler icinde tanimlayabilir, ama bir Stradivarius'un siradan bir kemandan NEDEN farkli ses cikardigini hala aciklayamaz — bu gizem ahsap, vernik ve 300 yillik yaslanmayi icerir.

summary_en: How the brain extracts the fundamental frequency from complex sounds — even when the fundamental is physically absent, your auditory system reconstructs it from the harmonic series.
summary_tr: Beynin karmasik seslerden temel frekansi nasil cikardigi — temel fiziksel olarak mevcut olmasa bile, isitsel sisteminiz onu harmonik seriden yeniden olusturur.
deep_en: Oxenham 2012: Two mechanisms operate in parallel — temporal (phase-locking up to ~4kHz) and spectral (resolved harmonics in the cochlea). The 'missing fundamental' illusion demonstrates that pitch is a neural construct, not a direct readout of physical frequency. Lateral Heschl's gyrus contains pitch-selective neurons. Musical training sharpens pitch discrimination by a factor of 6x.
deep_tr: Oxenham 2012: Iki mekanizma paralel calismaktadir — zamansal (~4kHz'e kadar faz kilitleme) ve spektral (kokleada cozulmus harmonikler). 'Kayip temel' illuzyonu perdenin fiziksel frekans dogrudan okumasindan degil noralal bir yapim oldugunu gosterir. Lateral Heschl girusu perdeye secici noronlar icerir. Muzikal egitim perde ayrimciligini 6 kat keskinlestirir.
fun_fact_en: You can hear the bass note in a phone speaker that physically cannot produce that frequency — your brain literally hallucinates the missing fundamental from the overtones.
fun_fact_tr: Fiziksel olarak o frekansi uretemeyen bir telefon hoparlöründen bas notayi duyabilirsiniz — beyniniz kelimenin tam anlamiyla ust harmoniklerden kayip temeli halusinasyon olarak uretir.

summary_en: The brain binds separate acoustic events into a single unified percept within ~200ms windows — the 'cognitive present' that determines whether two sounds are heard as one or two.
summary_tr: Beyin ayri akustik olaylari ~200ms'lik pencereler icinde tek bir birlesik algiya baglar — iki sesin bir mi yoksa iki mi olarak duyuldugunu belirleyen 'bilissel simdi'.
deep_en: Poeppel 2003: Auditory cortex oscillates in theta (~3-8Hz) and gamma (~30-80Hz) bands that create nested temporal integration windows. Theta defines the ~200ms 'perceptual moment', gamma handles fine-grained temporal binding within it. This maps directly to H3 micro horizons (H0-H7). Cross-frequency coupling between theta phase and gamma amplitude structures hierarchical temporal processing.
deep_tr: Poeppel 2003: Isitsel korteks, ic ice zamansal butunlestirme pencereleri olusturan teta (~3-8Hz) ve gama (~30-80Hz) bantlarinda salimlanir. Teta ~200ms'lik 'algisal ani' tanimlar, gama onun icindeki ince taneli zamansal baglmayi halleder. Bu dogrudan H3 mikro ufuklarina (H0-H7) haritalanir. Teta fazi ve gama genligi arasindaki capraz-frekans eslenmesi hiyerarsik zamansal islemeyi yapilandirir.
fun_fact_en: Your brain takes 'snapshots' of sound about 5 times per second — everything within each 200ms window gets fused into a single moment of experience.
fun_fact_tr: Beyniniz saniyede yaklasik 5 kez sesin 'anlik goruntusunu' alir — her 200ms'lik pencere icindeki her sey tek bir deneyim anina kaynasirilir.

summary_en: The nonlinear relationship between physical sound intensity and how loud we perceive it — doubling the energy only increases perceived loudness by about 30%.
summary_tr: Fiziksel ses siddeti ile onu ne kadar yuksek algiladigimiz arasindaki dogrusal olmayan iliski — enerjiyi ikiye katlamak algilanan ses yuksekligini yalnizca yaklasik %30 arttirir.
deep_en: Stevens 1957: Loudness follows a power law (sones = k * intensity^0.3). Equal-loudness contours (Fletcher-Munson curves) show frequency-dependent sensitivity — we're most sensitive at 2-5kHz (speech range). Dynamic range spans 120dB (~1 trillion-fold energy ratio). Loudness recruitment in hearing loss causes abnormal growth of loudness. NE modulates gain in auditory cortex during arousal.
deep_tr: Stevens 1957: Ses yuksekligi bir kuvvet yasasini izler (son = k * siddet^0.3). Esit ses yuksekligi konturlari (Fletcher-Munson egrileri) freansa baglam hassasiyet gosterir — 2-5kHz'de (konusma araligi) en hassasiz. Dinamik aralik 120dB'yi kapsar (~1 trilyon katlik enerji orani). Isitme kaybinda ses yuksekligi devsmesi anormal ses yuksekligi buyumesine neden olur. NE uyarilma sirasinda isitsel kortekste kazanci modüle eder.
fun_fact_en: Your ear can detect sounds that move the eardrum less than the diameter of a hydrogen atom — and still handle sounds a trillion times more powerful without breaking.
fun_fact_tr: Kulaginiz kulak zarinizi bir hidrojen atomunun capindan daha az hareket ettiren sesleri tespit edebilir — ve yine de bir trilyon kat daha guclu sesleri bozulmadan idare edebilir.

summary_en: The brain's reward hub where dopamine and opioid signals converge during musical pleasure — the same region that lights up for food, sex, and social bonding.
summary_tr: Muzikal haz sirasinda dopamin ve opioid sinyallerinin birlestigi beynin odul merkezi — yemek, cinsellik ve sosyal baglanma icin de aktive olan ayni bolge.
deep_en: Salimpoor 2011: PET imaging shows DA release in NAcc during peak musical pleasure (chills). NAcc receives converging inputs from VTA (DA), ventral pallidum (OPI), amygdala (salience), and hippocampus (memory context). In C3, NAcc is part of the reward processing unit (RPU) within F6, computing hedonic value from prediction error magnitude and familiarity modulation.
deep_tr: Salimpoor 2011: PET goruntuleme doruk muzikal haz (urperti) sirasinda NAcc'de DA salimini gosterir. NAcc VTA'dan (DA), ventral pallidumdan (OPI), amigdaladan (belirginlik) ve hipokampusten (hafiza baglami) yaklasan girdiler alir. C3'de NAcc, tahmin hatasi buyuklugu ve asinalik modulasyonundan hedonik deger hesaplayan F6 icerisindeki odul isleme biriminin (RPU) parcasidir.
fun_fact_en: When you get chills from music, your nucleus accumbens releases dopamine in the exact same pattern as when anticipating your favorite food — your brain literally treats great music as a biological reward.
fun_fact_tr: Muzikten urpertiginde nukleus akkumbensiniz en sevdigin yemegi beklerkenki ile tamamen ayni oruntude dopamin saliyor — beyniniz harika muzigi kelimenin tam anlamiyla biyolojik bir odul olarak goruyor.

summary_en: The primary sound processing hub in the temporal lobe, organized tonotopically — where raw acoustic signals are transformed into meaningful musical representations.
summary_tr: Temporal lobdaki birincil ses isleme merkezi, tonotopik olarak duzenlenmis — ham akustik sinyallerin anlamli muzikal temsillere donusturuldugu yer.
deep_en: Zatorre 2002: Primary auditory cortex (A1, Heschl's gyrus) performs spectrotemporal analysis. Belt and parabelt regions extract higher-order features: pitch (lateral HG), melody (anterior STG), rhythm (posterior STG). Musicians show 130% larger cortical volume in Heschl's gyrus. Right hemisphere dominates for spectral/melodic processing, left for temporal/rhythmic. In C3, auditory cortex maps to the SPU relay in F1.
deep_tr: Zatorre 2002: Primer isitsel korteks (A1, Heschl girusu) spektrotemporal analiz yapar. Kemere ve parakemere bölgeleri yuksek duzeyli ozellikler cikarir: perde (lateral HG), melodi (anterior STG), ritim (posterior STG). Muzisyenler Heschl girusunda %130 daha buyuk kortikal hacim gosterir. Sag yarikure spektral/melodik isleme icin baskindir, sol zamansal/ritmik icin. C3'de isitsel korteks F1'deki SPU rolesine haritalanir.
fun_fact_en: A professional musician's auditory cortex can be 130% larger than a non-musician's — years of practice physically sculpt the brain's sound processing hardware.
fun_fact_tr: Profesyonel bir muzisyenin isitsel korteksi muzisyen olmayanlarinkinden %130 daha buyuk olabilir — yillarca pratik beynin ses isleme donaninini fiziksel olarak sekillendiriyor.

summary_en: The brain's emotional significance detector — tags musical events as important, responds to dissonance, unexpected harmonies, and emotional peaks in music.
summary_tr: Beynin duygusal onem dedektoru — muzikal olaylari onemli olarak isaretler, uyumsuzluga, beklenmedik harmonilere ve muzikteki duygusal doruklara yanit verir.
deep_en: Koelsch 2014: Amygdala responds to musical dissonance, unexpected chord progressions, and emotionally salient musical features. Bilateral activation for fearful/threatening music, left lateralized for sad music. Direct connections to autonomic nervous system mediate physiological responses (heart rate, skin conductance). In C3, amygdala is part of the ASU relay in F3, contributing to salience-weighted attention.
deep_tr: Koelsch 2014: Amigdala muzikal uyumsuzluga, beklenmedik akor ilerlemelerine ve duygusal olarak belirgin muzikal ozelliklere yanit verir. Korkutucu/tehditkâr muzik icin bilateral aktivasyon, huzunlu muzik icin sol lateralize. Otonom sinir sistemine dogrudan baglantilar fizyolojik yanitlara (kalp hizi, deri iletkenl) aracilik eder. C3'de amigdala F3'deki ASU rolesine dahildir ve belirginlik agirlikli dikkate katkida bulunur.
fun_fact_en: Your amygdala responds to a dissonant chord in under 200ms — faster than you can consciously recognize the chord, your brain has already flagged it as emotionally significant.
fun_fact_tr: Amigdalaniz uyumsuz bir akora 200ms'nin altinda yanit verir — akoru bilincinizle tanimadan once beyniniz onu duygusal olarak onemli diye coktan isaretlemistir.

summary_en: The brain's memory binding center — crucial for connecting music to autobiographical memories, encoding new musical experiences, and the powerful nostalgia that familiar songs evoke.
summary_tr: Beynin hafiza baglama merkezi — muzigi otobiyografik anilara baglamak, yeni muzikal deneyimleri kodlamak ve tanidik sarkilarin uyandirdigi guclu nostalji icin hayati oneme sahip.
deep_en: Janata 2009: Music-evoked autobiographical memories (MEAMs) show robust hippocampal-vmPFC co-activation. Hippocampus binds musical features (melody, timbre, tempo) with contextual details (place, person, emotion) into episodic traces. Preserved in early Alzheimer's (Cuddy & Duffin 2005) due to relatively late hippocampal atrophy. In C3, hippocampus maps to the IMU relay in F4, supporting memory encoding and retrieval.
deep_tr: Janata 2009: Muzikle uyandirilan otobiyografik anilar (MEAM'lar) guclu hipokampal-vmPFC birlikte aktivasyonu gosterir. Hipokampus muzikal ozellikleri (melodi, timbre, tempo) baglamsal detaylarla (yer, kisi, duygu) epizodik izlere baglar. Erken Alzheimer'da korunur (Cuddy & Duffin 2005) nispeten gec hipokampal atrofi nedeniyle. C3'de hipokampus F4'deki IMU rolesine haritalanir, hafiza kodlama ve hatirlamayi destekler.
fun_fact_en: Alzheimer's patients who can't remember their own children's names can still sing along perfectly to songs from their youth — musical memories are stored in circuits that resist neurodegeneration.
fun_fact_tr: Kendi cocuklarinin isimlerini hatirlayamayan Alzheimer hastalari gencliklerindeki sarkilara hala mukemmel eslik edebilir — muzikal anilar norodejenerasyona direnen devrelerde saklanir.

summary_en: The hidden cortical island that bridges body sensations and conscious feeling — transforms musical sounds into felt bodily experiences like chills, tension, and the urge to move.
summary_tr: Beden duyumlari ile bilinçli hissi birbirine baglayan gizli kortikal ada — muzikal sesleri urperti, gerilim ve hareket etme durtusü gibi hissedilen bedensel deneyimlere donusturur.
deep_en: Craig 2009: Anterior insula integrates interoceptive signals with emotional awareness, creating the 'feeling of knowing'. During musical chills, insula co-activates with NAcc and ACC. Insula mediates the embodied quality of musical experience — why music 'moves' us physically. In C3, insula contributes to the MPU relay in F7, bridging motor-affective integration.
deep_tr: Craig 2009: Anterior insula interoseptif sinyalleri duygusal farkindalikla butunlestirerek 'bilme hissi' yaratir. Muzikal urpertiler sirasinda insula NAcc ve ACC ile birlikte aktive olur. Insula muzikal deneyimin bedenlesnis niteligine aracilik eder — muzigin bizi fiziksel olarak neden 'hareket ettirdigi'. C3'de insula F7'deki MPU rolesine katkida bulunur, motor-afektif entegrasyonu kopruler.
fun_fact_en: The insula is why you feel music 'in your gut' — it literally maps your body's internal state onto your conscious experience of sound.
fun_fact_tr: Insua muzigi 'icinden hissetmenin' nedenidir — bedeninizin ic durumunu kelimenin tam anlamiyla bilinçli ses deneyiminize haritalandirir.

summary_en: A brain network active during mind-wandering, daydreaming, and internal imagination — music engages it powerfully, enabling visual imagery, personal memories, and the 'movie in your mind'.
summary_tr: Zihin gezintisi, hayal kurma ve ic imgelem sirasinda aktif olan bir beyin agi — muzik onu guclu bir sekilde devreye sokar, gorsel imgelem, kisisel anilar ve 'zihindeki filmi' mumkun kilar.
deep_en: Raichle 2001: DMN includes mPFC, PCC, angular gyrus, and hippocampal formation. During music listening, DMN shows unique activation patterns — not suppressed as during most external tasks. Familiar music activates DMN more than unfamiliar (Wilkins 2014). Musical mind-wandering correlates with stronger DMN-auditory cortex functional connectivity. Supports self-referential processing during music.
deep_tr: Raichle 2001: DMN mPFC, PCC, angular girus ve hipokampal olusumu icerir. Muzik dinleme sirasinda DMN benzersiz aktivasyon oruntuleri gosterir — cogu dis gorev sirasinda oldugu gibi bastirilmaz. Tanidik muzik bilinmeyenden daha fazla DMN'yi aktive eder (Wilkins 2014). Muzikal zihin gezintisi daha guclu DMN-isitsel korteks fonksiyonel baglantiligi ile koreledir. Muzik sirasinda oz-referansli islemeyi destekler.
fun_fact_en: Most tasks shut down the brain's daydreaming network, but music is special — it's one of the few external stimuli that actually turns ON your imagination circuits.
fun_fact_tr: Cogu gorev beynin hayal kurma agini kapatir, ama muzik ozeldir — hayal gucunuz devrelerini gercekten ACAN birkaç dis uyarandan biridir.

summary_en: A mathematical measure of uncertainty in musical sequences — high entropy means many possible continuations (jazz improvisation), low entropy means few (a familiar nursery rhyme).
summary_tr: Muzikal dizilerdeki belirsizligin matematiksel olcusu — yuksek entropi birçok olasi devam anlama gelir (caz dogaclamasi), dusuk entropi az anlama gelir (tanidik bir ninni).
deep_en: Shannon 1948: H(X) = -sum P(x) log2 P(x). In music, entropy quantifies how predictable the next note/chord is given the context. Pearce 2005 (IDyOM): high entropy contexts generate larger N1/P2 ERP amplitudes. Entropy tracks emotional tension — increasing entropy correlates with arousal. C3 uses entropy as a key input to prediction error computation in F2.
deep_tr: Shannon 1948: H(X) = -toplam P(x) log2 P(x). Muzikte entropi, baglam verildignde sonraki notanin/akorun ne kadar tahmin edilebilir oldugunu niceler. Pearce 2005 (IDyOM): yuksek entropi baglamlari daha buyuk N1/P2 ERP genlikleri uretir. Entropi duygusal gerilimi izler — artan entropi uyarilma ile koreledir. C3 entropiyi F2'de tahmin hatasi hesaplamasina anahtar girdi olarak kullanir.
fun_fact_en: A pop chorus has about 2 bits of entropy per note, while a free jazz solo can reach 5+ bits — that's why jazz feels 'harder to follow' and pop feels 'catchy'.
fun_fact_tr: Bir pop nakaratinin nota basina yaklasik 2 bit entropisi varken, serbest caz solosu 5+ bit'e ulasabilir — caz'in 'takip etmesi zor' ve pop'un 'akilda kalici' hissettirmesinin nedeni budur.

summary_en: A statistical model where the probability of the next musical event depends only on the current state — the mathematical backbone of how your brain predicts upcoming notes and chords.
summary_tr: Bir sonraki muzikal olayin olasiliginin yalnizca mevcut duruma bagdli oldugu istatistiksel bir model — beyninizin gelecek notalari ve akorlari nasil tahmin ettignin matematiksel omurgasi.
deep_en: Pearce & Wiggins 2012: Variable-order Markov models capture musical style statistics at multiple context lengths simultaneously. IDyOM uses PPM* (prediction by partial matching) to learn note-to-note transition probabilities. First-order captures local dependencies, higher orders capture phrase-level patterns. Brain's prediction error correlates with Markov model surprise (IC = -log2 P). C3 F2 uses Markov-like temporal prediction in the HTP mechanism.
deep_tr: Pearce & Wiggins 2012: Degisken sirali Markov modelleri muzikal tarz istatistiklerini ayni anda birden fazla baglam uzunluugunda yakalar. IDyOM nota-nota gecis olasiliklarini ogrenmek icin PPM* (kismi eslestirme ile tahmin) kullanir. Birinci sira yerel bagimliliklari, daha yuksek siralar cümle düzeyi oruntuleri yakalar. Beynin tahmin hatasi Markov model surpriziyle koreledir (IC = -log2 P). C3 F2 HTP mekanizmasinda Markov benzeri zamansal tahmin kullanir.
fun_fact_en: Most Western pop songs can be predicted with 70% accuracy using just a 3rd-order Markov chain — proof that pop music really does follow predictable patterns.
fun_fact_tr: Cogu Bati pop sarkisi sadece 3. dereceden bir Markov zinciri kullanilarak %70 dogrulukla tahmin edilebilir — pop muzigin gercekten tahmin edilebilir kaliplari izlediginin kaniti.

summary_en: The C3 mechanism that processes musical features across different time scales simultaneously — from micro (50ms attack) to ultra (minutes-long form), each horizon reveals different patterns.
summary_tr: Muzikal ozellikleri farkli zaman olceklerinde es zamanli islyen C3 mekanizmasi — mikro'dan (50ms atak) ultra'ya (dakikalar suren form), her ufuk farkli oruntuleri ortaya cikarir.
deep_en: H3 architecture: 32 horizons across 4 scales (Micro H0-H7, Meso H8-H15, Macro H16-H23, Ultra H24-H31). Each horizon applies 24 morphological transforms (mean, std, velocity, periodicity, trend, etc.) across 3 temporal laws (backward/causal L0, forward L1, bidirectional L2). Total theoretical space: 97x32x24x3 = 223,488 tuples, ~8,600 active. This multi-scale representation captures everything from individual note attacks to movement-level structure.
deep_tr: H3 mimarisi: 4 olcek boyunca 32 ufuk (Mikro H0-H7, Mezo H8-H15, Makro H16-H23, Ultra H24-H31). Her ufuk 3 zamansal yasa (geriye/nedensel L0, ileri L1, cift yonlu L2) boyunca 24 morfolojik donosum uygular (ortalama, std, hiz, periyodiklik, egilim, vb.). Toplam teorik uzay: 97x32x24x3 = 223,488 tuple, ~8,600 aktif. Bu coklu olcek temsili bireysel nota ataklarindan bolum duzeyinde yapiya kadar her seyi yakalar.
fun_fact_en: Your brain simultaneously tracks a note's 50ms attack AND a symphony's 40-minute arc — C3 models this with 32 nested time horizons running in parallel.
fun_fact_tr: Beyniniz bir notanin 50ms'lik atagini VE bir senfoninin 40 dakikalik yayini es zamanli olarak izler — C3 bunu paralel calisan 32 ic ice zaman ufkuyla modellemektedir.

summary_en: A mathematical method where recent events carry more weight than older ones, with influence decaying exponentially — how C3 models the brain's fading memory of past musical events.
summary_tr: Yakin olaylarin eskilerinden daha fazla agirlik tasidigi, etkinin ustel olarak azaldigi matematiksel bir yontem — C3'un beynin gecmis muzikal olaylara iliskin solan hafizasini nasil modelledigi.
deep_en: EMA (Exponential Moving Average): S_t = alpha * x_t + (1-alpha) * S_{t-1}. Alpha controls the decay rate — high alpha = responsive to recent events, low alpha = smooth/slow adaptation. H3 uses EMA within each horizon to compute temporal morphologies. Different horizons use different alpha values, creating the multi-scale temporal representation. This mirrors neural adaptation time constants in auditory cortex.
deep_tr: EMA (Ustel Hareketli Ortalama): S_t = alfa * x_t + (1-alfa) * S_{t-1}. Alfa bozunma hizini kontrol eder — yuksek alfa = yakin olaylara duyarli, dusuk alfa = yumusak/yavas adaptasyon. H3 zamansal morfolojileri hesaplamak icin her ufuk icinde EMA kullanir. Farkli ufuklar farkli alfa degerleri kullanarak coklu olcek zamansal temsili olusturur. Bu isitsel korteksteki noralal adaptasyon zaman sabitlerini yansitir.
fun_fact_en: Your brain doesn't weight all past events equally — the last 2 seconds of music influence your prediction 10x more than what happened 20 seconds ago.
fun_fact_tr: Beyniniz tum gecmis olaylari esit agirliklandirmaz — son 2 saniyelik muzik tahmininizi 20 saniye once olandan 10 kat daha fazla etkiler.

summary_en: C3's core mechanism for weighting prediction errors by their reliability — a precise prediction error matters more than a noisy one, allowing the brain to separate signal from noise.
summary_tr: C3'un tahmin hatalarini guvenilirliklerine gore agirliklandirma temel mekanizmasi — hassas bir tahmin hatasi gurultulu olandan daha onemlidir, beynin sinyali gurultudan ayirmasini saglar.
deep_en: Clark 2013: Precision-weighted prediction error (PWPE) = gain * PE, where gain = pi_obs/(pi_obs + pi_pred). C3 implementation: precision = tanh(pi_raw/12), clamped to [0,1]. High precision = confident observation → large update. Low precision = uncertain observation → small update. This prevents the system from overreacting to noise while remaining sensitive to reliable signals. Precision is modulated by attention (NE), familiarity (5HT), and context (DA).
deep_tr: Clark 2013: Hassasiyet-agirlikli tahmin hatasi (PWPE) = kazanc * TH, burada kazanc = pi_obs/(pi_obs + pi_pred). C3 uygulamasi: hassasiyet = tanh(pi_raw/12), [0,1]'e kisitlanmis. Yuksek hassasiyet = guvenli gozlem → buyuk guncelleme. Dusuk hassasiyet = belirsiz gozlem → kucuk guncelleme. Bu sistemin gurultuye asiri tepki vermesini onlerken guvenilir sinyallere duyarli kalmasini saglar. Hassasiyet dikkat (NE), asinalik (5HT) ve baglam (DA) tarafindan modüle edilir.
fun_fact_en: Your brain doesn't just track what's surprising — it tracks how CONFIDENT it should be in each surprise. A wrong note in a quiet solo matters more than one buried in a loud orchestra.
fun_fact_tr: Beyniniz sadece neyin sasirtici oldugunu izlemez — her surprize ne kadar GUVENMESI gerektigini de izler. Sessiz bir solodaki yanlis nota, gurultulu bir orkestra icine gomulu olandan daha cok onem tasir.

summary_en: Age-dependent windows when the brain is maximally receptive to musical training — starting before age 7 produces measurably different brain architecture than starting later.
summary_tr: Beynin muzikal egitime maksimum duzeyde acik oldugu yasa bagli pencereler — 7 yasindan once baslamak daha gec baslamaktan olculebilir sekilde farkli beyin mimarisi uretir.
deep_en: Schlaug 2005: Musicians who began training before age 7 show significantly larger corpus callosum (anterior portion) than those who started later — even controlling for total years of training. Absolute pitch acquisition almost exclusively occurs with training onset before age 6. However, musical plasticity continues throughout life — adult beginners still show cortical changes after 15 months (Hyde 2009). The critical period reflects heightened synaptic plasticity, not a hard cutoff.
deep_tr: Schlaug 2005: 7 yasindan once egitme baslayan muzisyenler, toplam egitim yillari kontrol edildiginde bile, daha gec baslayanlara gore onemli olcude daha buyuk korpus kallosum (anterior bolum) gosterir. Mutlak perde edinimi neredeyse yalnizca 6 yasindan once egitim baslandiginda gercelesir. Ancak muzikal plastisite yasam boyunca devam eder — yetiskin yeni baslayanlar 15 ay sonra bile kortikal degisiklikler gosterir (Hyde 2009). Kritik donem yukselmis sinaptik plastisiteyi yansitir, kesin bir kesim noktasi degil.
fun_fact_en: Starting piano before age 7 literally builds a bigger bridge between your brain's hemispheres — the corpus callosum grows extra fibers that last a lifetime.
fun_fact_tr: 7 yasindan once piyanoya baslamak kelimenin tam anlamiyla beyin yarikurelerin arasinda daha buyuk bir kopru insa eder — korpus kallosum omur boyu suren ekstra lifler buyutur.

summary_en: How musical training benefits far beyond music itself — improved language processing, mathematical reasoning, executive function, and even social skills.
summary_tr: Muzikal egitimin muzigin otesine nasil fayda sagladigi — gelismis dil isleme, matematiksel akil yurutme, yonetici islevler ve hatta sosyal beceriler.
deep_en: Kraus & Chandrasekaran 2010: Musical training enhances brainstem encoding of speech sounds (FFR). Moreno 2009: 6 months of music lessons improved reading and pitch discrimination in speech more than painting lessons. Schellenberg 2004: Music lessons raised IQ by 2-3 points over controls (drama/no lessons). Mechanism: shared neural resources for music and language in IFG, STG, and premotor cortex. Executive function gains from managing complex motor sequences under time pressure.
deep_tr: Kraus & Chandrasekaran 2010: Muzikal egitim konusma seslerinin beyin sapi kodlamasini (FFR) gelistirir. Moreno 2009: 6 aylik muzik dersleri okuma ve konusmada perde ayrimciligini resim derslerinden daha fazla gelistirdi. Schellenberg 2004: Muzik dersleri kontrollere (drama/ders yok) gore IQ'yu 2-3 puan yükseltti. Mekanizma: IFG, STG ve premotor kortekste muzik ve dil icin paylasilan noralal kaynaklar. Zaman baskisi altinda karmasik motor dizileri yonetmekten yonetici islev kazanimlari.
fun_fact_en: Children who take music lessons for just 6 months show better reading skills than those who take painting lessons — music literally tunes your brain for language.
fun_fact_tr: Sadece 6 ay muzik dersi alan cocuklar resim dersi alanlardan daha iyi okuma becerileri gosterir — muzik kelimenin tam anlamiyla beyninizi dil icin akort eder.

summary_en: From casual listener to concert virtuoso, musical expertise exists on a continuum — each level shows measurably different brain structure, function, and perceptual ability.
summary_tr: Siradan dinleyiciden konser virtuozune, muzikal uzmanlik bir sureklilik uzerinde var olur — her duzey olculebilir sekilde farkli beyin yapisi, isevi ve algisal yetenek gosterir.
deep_en: Munte et al. 2002: Expertise manifests as: (1) enhanced auditory cortex volume and tonotopic precision, (2) stronger MMN responses to deviants, (3) faster and more accurate sensorimotor integration, (4) richer harmonic expectation (larger ERAN to unexpected chords). 10,000 hours of deliberate practice (Ericsson 1993) produces qualitatively different neural architecture. However, passive listening expertise also produces measurable effects — 'audiophiles' show enhanced spectral processing.
deep_tr: Munte et al. 2002: Uzmanlik su sekilde tezahur eder: (1) gelismis isitsel korteks hacmi ve tonotopik hassasiyet, (2) sapmalara daha guclu MMN yanitlari, (3) daha hizli ve daha doğru duyusal-motor entegrasyonu, (4) daha zengin harmonik beklenti (beklenmedik akorlara daha buyuk ERAN). 10.000 saatlik kasitli pratik (Ericsson 1993) nitel olarak farkli noralal mimari uretir. Ancak pasif dinleme uzmanligi da olculebilir etkiler uretir — 'audiofiller' gelismis spektral isleme gosterir.
fun_fact_en: Even without playing an instrument, 'expert listeners' who consume 30+ hours of music per week show measurably enhanced auditory processing — your ears get sharper just from dedicated listening.
fun_fact_tr: Bir enstruman calmadan bile haftada 30+ saat muzik tuketeki 'uzman dinleyiciler' olculebilir sekilde gelismis isitsel isleme gosterir — kulaklariniz sadece adanmis dinlemeyle bile keskinlesir.

summary_en: The gradual sharpening of auditory discrimination through exposure and practice — your ear learns to detect smaller and smaller differences in pitch, timing, and timbre.
summary_tr: Maruziyet ve pratik yoluyla isitsel ayrim kabiliyetinin kademeli olarak keskinlesmesi — kulagınız perde, zamanlama ve tınıdaki giderek daha kucuk farkliliklari tespit etmeyi ogrenir.
deep_en: Goldstone 1998: Perceptual learning involves (1) attentional weighting — learning which features matter, (2) differentiation — formerly identical stimuli become discriminable, (3) unitization — complex patterns become single percepts. In auditory cortex, perceptual learning narrows neuronal tuning curves and increases population coding efficiency. Wine tasters of sound: trained listeners discriminate 1/6-tone pitch differences vs 1/2-tone for untrained.
deep_tr: Goldstone 1998: Algisal ogrenme (1) dikkat agirliklama — hangi ozelliklerin onemli oldugunu ogrenme, (2) farklilastirma — eskiden ozdes uyaranlar ayirt edilebilir hale gelir, (3) birlestirme — karmasik orüntüler tek algiya donusur icerir. Isitsel kortekste algisal ogrenme noron ayar egrilerini daraltir ve populasyon kodlama verimliligini arttirir. Sesin sarap taticilari: egitimli dinleyiciler egitimsizler icin 1/2-ton'a karsi 1/6-ton perde farklarini ayirt eder.
fun_fact_en: After just 8 hours of pitch training, your brain rewires enough to hear differences half the size of what you could detect before — your ears are far more trainable than you think.
fun_fact_tr: Sadece 8 saatlik perde egitiminden sonra beyniniz onceden tespit edebildiginizin yari buyuklugundeki farklari duyacak kadar yeniden baglanir — kulaklariniz dusundugünuzden çok daha egitlebilir.

summary_en: How sleep strengthens and stabilizes musical memory traces — motor sequences practiced before sleep are performed better after waking, even without additional practice.
summary_tr: Uykunun muzikal hafiza izlerini nasil guclendirip stabilize ettigi — uyku oncesi calisan motor dizileri ek pratik olmadan bile uyaniktan sonra daha iyi icra edilir.
deep_en: Walker 2005: Sleep-dependent consolidation involves hippocampal replay during NREM slow-wave sleep (SWS) and spindle-mediated transfer to neocortex. Musical motor sequences show 20% performance improvement after sleep vs equivalent wake period. REM sleep may consolidate creative/associative aspects of musical memory. Sleep spindles correlate with declarative memory for musical structure. Emotional music memories are preferentially consolidated during REM.
deep_tr: Walker 2005: Uykuya bagli pekistirme NREM yavas dalga uykusu (SWS) sirasinda hipokampal tekrar oynatma ve ig-aracili neokortekse aktarimi icerir. Muzikal motor dizileri esdeğer uyanik döneme kiyasla uyku sonrasinda %20 performans iyilestirmesi gosterir. REM uykusu muzikal hafizanin yaratici/cagrisimsal yonlerini pekistirebilir. Uyku igleri muzikal yapi icin bildirimsel hafiza ile koreledir. Duygusal muzik anilari REM sirasinda tercihli olarak pekistirilir.
fun_fact_en: Musicians who 'sleep on it' after practicing a difficult passage perform 20% better the next morning — your brain literally rehearses while you sleep.
fun_fact_tr: Zor bir pasaji calistiktan sonra 'ustuüne uyuyan' muzisyenler ertesi sabah %20 daha iyi performans gösterir — beyniniz siz uyurken kelimenin tam anlamiyla prova yapar.

summary_en: Building mental frameworks for musical styles through exposure — after enough listening, your brain creates templates that define what 'sounds right' for jazz, classical, pop, or any genre.
summary_tr: Maruziyet yoluyla muzikal tarzlar icin zihinsel cerceveler olusturma — yeterli dinlemeden sonra beyniniz caz, klasik, pop veya herhangi bir tur icin 'dogru sesi' tanimlayan sablonlar yaratir.
deep_en: Bharucha 1987: Musical schemas encode style-specific distributional statistics (key profiles, chord transition probabilities, metric templates). Schemas are hierarchical — from low-level (interval expectations) to high-level (formal structure). Violation of schemas produces ERAN ERP component (early right anterior negativity). Schema acquisition follows statistical learning principles but consolidates into rigid templates over extended exposure. Cross-cultural studies show schemas are culture-specific, not innate.
deep_tr: Bharucha 1987: Muzikal semalar tarza ozgu dagilimsal istatistikleri kodlar (anahtar profilleri, akor gecis olasiliklari, metrik sablonlar). Semalar hiyerarsiktir — dusuk duzeyden (aralik beklentileri) yuksek duzeye (bicimsel yapi). Semalarin ihlali ERAN ERP bileseni uretir (erken sag anterior negatiflik). Sema edinimi istatistiksel ogrenme ilkelerini izler ama uzun sureli maruziyetle katı sablonlara pekisir. Kulturler arasi calismalar semalarin kulturel olarak ozel oldugunu, dogumsal olmadigini gosterir.
fun_fact_en: After about 200 hours of listening to a new genre, your brain builds a statistical model good enough to 'feel' when something sounds wrong — that gut feeling IS your schema.
fun_fact_tr: Yeni bir ture yaklasik 200 saat dinlemeden sonra beyniniz bir seyin yanlis seslendigini 'hissetmeye' yetecek kadar iyi bir istatistiksel model kurar — o ic his semanizin TA KENDISIDIR.
